founder.name: Niloy Saha
founder.short_bio: Visionary founder dedicated to empowering Bangladeshi youth.
founder.bio:
Meet Niloy Saha, a passionate public speaker, policy advocate, and development enthusiast from Bangladesh. He is currently pursuing a BA in Philosophy at the University of Rajshahi. With over six years of hands-on experience, Niloy focuses on civic innovation, digital inclusion, and governance through youth-led initiatives that empower communities.

As the Founder and CEO of the Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum (BSCF) and a lead in Youth for Policy (YFP), Niloy has guided diverse teams in projects that build digital skills and promote ethical online engagement. His key efforts include leading the Digital Citizenship and Project Shore O initiatives, where he helped more than 100 young people in Bangladesh gain essential tools for civic tech and digital democracy.

Niloy brings strong skills in project management, monitoring and evaluation, research, communication, grant writing, and team leadership. He has partnered with civil society groups, run workshops, and advocated for inclusive governance. His background in philosophy strengthens his ethical approach to supporting marginalized voices.

Honored with fellowships like Youth for Policy and national awards such as the President Scout Award, Niloy stays committed to advancing democratic values through local and regional work in South Asia. His career goals include leading civic tech programs, applying his expertise in grant-making and advocacy, coordinating peer learning, and contributing to organizations that promote accountability and sustainable digital governance.

coreTeam.name: Md. Reashad Akhter
coreTeam.bio: Meet Md. Reashad Akhter, a skilled brand manager and marketing professional with expertise in international marketing, communications, and event management from Bangladesh. He holds an MBA in Marketing from Bangladesh University of Professionals and a BBA in Marketing with a minor in Human Resource Management from the same institution. Currently serving as Brand Manager for International Marketing at SQUARE Food & Beverage Ltd. since June 2022, he spearheads product development, event planning, data analysis, audits, promotions, trademark processes, and packaging management for global markets. Previously, as Advertising and Communication Secretary at Star T Vision and Event from March 2018 to April 2020, he developed campaigns, created promotional materials, coordinated teams, and managed social media to enhance event visibility. Proficient in maintaining effective communications, promotional activities, advertising, and cultural affairs, Reashad aims to drive brand growth and strategic marketing initiatives in the food and beverage sector.

coreTeam.name: Rafia Jebin
coreTeam.bio: Meet Rafia Jabin, a sociology graduate with a strong base in social research, policy analysis, and community engagement from Dhaka, Bangladesh. She completed her BA in Sociology at Begum Badrunnessa Government Girl's College. Since June 2024, Rafia has worked as a business intern at Logistica, applying her skills to support operations. As director of Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum and vice president of Rajshahi Socio-Cultural Forum, she drives initiatives for cultural and social awareness. She participated in a workshop on youth engagement and democracy focused on right to information by Manusher Jonno Foundation. Her interests include community empowerment, social justice, grassroots development, participatory governance, and climate justice. Rafia aims to contribute to development programs that empower marginalized groups and promote equity, sustainability, and inclusive growth through her academic insights and field experience.

coreTeam.name: Samin Yasar Saad
coreTeam.bio: Meet Samin Yasar Saad, a dedicated mechanical engineer and development enthusiast from Chattogram, Bangladesh. He graduated with a Bachelor's degree in Mechanical Engineering from Rajshahi University of Engineering & Technology, where he studied from December 2019 to July 2025. His research centers on metamaterials and material-engineering challenges, using finite element analysis and experimental testing to turn simulations into validated hardware. He contributed to vehicle development with Team Annexe RUET in the Formula Student program, handling assembly, systems integration, and test driving to understand design behavior under real conditions. Since January 2024, Samin has served as Co-Lead at Youth for Policy, managing leadership and team coordination for community impact initiatives and policy advocacy. Samin also holds position as the Director (Administration) of Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum (BSCF). In March 2024, he interned at GPH Ispat, working on rolling mill maintenance and operations, also contributing to quality control assurance and ensuring safety measures. From March to April 2023, he interned at Karnafuly Shipbuilders Limited, focusing on propeller inspection. His projects include the Formula Kart Design Challenge Season 7 in 2023, providing technical support as a technical team crew, as well as pilot of the vehicle. Samin also holds certifications as Electrical Co-Lead for Team Annexe RUET and a workshop on Basic FESEM Training from BUET. He volunteered as Chartered President of Leo Club of Chittagong Solidarity from 2018 to 2023 and as a member of Lions Clubs International from 2023 to 2024. His skills include AutoCAD, research, leadership, quality assurance, automotive work, vehicle assembly, and on-track driving. He published a 2024 ICMIME conference paper on mechanical properties of Gerbera stem-inspired dodecagonal structures, available on ResearchGate. Samin aims to apply his engineering expertise and event coordination skills to create impactful community solutions while pursuing mechanical engineering roles.

coreTeam.name: Redwan Ahmed
coreTeam.bio: Meet Redwan Ahmed, a motivated graduate and development enthusiast from Rajshahi, Bangladesh. He holds a BA in Philosophy from the University of Rajshahi. Since December 2024, he has served as a business intern at Logistica, applying his skills to support operations. In Project Sware-O with Team Nirvana, he helped enhance digital literacy for youth in Rajshahi to build a smarter Bangladesh. His achievements include first runner-up in Policy Hackathon 2024, participation in Youth Policy Summit 2024, Policy CampX, inter-year debate competition, and a workshop on youth engagement and democracy by Manusher Jonno Foundation. As general secretary of Rajshahi University Philosophy Students' Alliance, treasurer for Youth for Policy's Rajshahi District Committee, director of events at Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum, lead in administration and procurement for Team Nirvana, media secretary at Rajshahi University Photographic Club, and former associate in groups like Rajshahi University Model United Nations Association and SILSWA, he fosters leadership, research, and community activities. Skilled in policy analysis, document management, digital literacy course management, education, and project management, Redwan aims to contribute to sustainable growth and social impact through inclusive and equitable development solutions.

coreTeam.name: Aurit Ahsan Ohi
coreTeam.bio: Meet Aurit Ahsan, a passionate and driven law graduate with a focus on leadership, policy discourse, and advocacy for human rights, liberty, and social change from Rajshahi, Bangladesh. He holds a Bachelor of Laws (LL.B. Honors) from the Department of Law at the University of Rajshahi. As Local Coordinator for South Asia at Students for Liberty since 2025, he leads the largest international pro-liberty student organization, selected from over 1,500 applicants in Bangladesh. He is also a Fellow in the Commonwealth Asia Youth Alliance Advocacy Fellowship since 2025, chosen from over 1,000 applicants across Asia. Aurit participated in the UMSAILS-UAP Spring School 2024 on forced displacement and volunteered as Head of Communication for the International Conference on Human Rights and Social Justice 2023, coordinating teams and hundreds of participants. As Senior Vice-President of Rajshahi University Debating Organization since 2023, he has conducted numerous events. His achievements include acceptance into Cohort 4 of the 2025 Aspire Leadership Program by Harvard University, winning the Best Ad-hoc Committee award at the NILS-LEB National Model Legislative Assembly 2025, selection for the Youth Policy Summit 2024, and a full scholarship for the UMSAILS-UAP Spring School. With interests in debating, advocacy, youth activism, graphics designing, event management, volunteerism, and motorsports, Aurit brings a globally aware perspective to advance human rights through legal research and youth initiatives.

coreTeam.name: Arabinda Banerjee Anik
coreTeam.bio:
Meet Arabinda Banerjee, an undergraduate physics student and active volunteer in social and policy initiatives from Rajshahi, Bangladesh.

For about eight months, Arabinda has been Assistant Director of Finance at Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum (BSCF), where he managed finances, organized events, planned workshops on case solving, directed the Divisional Astronomy Camp 2024, led a fundraising team for flood relief, and handled sponsorships for the Future STEM Leaders Program Cohort-1. As Volunteer Coordinator for Youth For Policy-Rajshahi Division for around ten months, he formed an Anti-Sexual Harassment Committee at Rajshahi University School, created a documentary for International Mother Language Day, organized a Gender Equality session at Rajshahi University of Engineering & Technology, and helped with the Youth Policy Summit at Dhaka University.

Professionally, he managed trainer enrollments at Kore Search in Dhaka from November 2024 to June 2025. As Campus Ambassador for SheSTEM for one month, he advanced women's careers by collecting CVs, placing candidates in jobs and internships, and signed an MOU between SheSTEM and BSCF for programs like the Future STEM Leaders and Divisional Astronomy Camp 2025. 

Passionate about cricket, debate, and case competitions, he broke into the top 16 in RUDF Fresher's Championship, reached semifinals in BrandEdge 2025 and Sellsation 2023, and was a semifinalist in an interdepartmental debate on Free Market Environmentalism. Arabinda enjoys music, business, books, and friends, aiming to grow in business and social impact.

coreTeam.name: Sadia Nowshin Sharin
coreTeam.bio:
Nowshin Sharin is a young professional dedicated to advancing cultural dialogue and community development. As the Assistant Director of External Affairs at the Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum (BSCF), she actively works to strengthen partnerships, promote Bangladeshi heritage, and engage youth in creative, socially impactful initiatives.

A graduate of Daffodil International University, Nowshin brings a strong background in communication, media, and organizational management. Alongside her role at BSCF, she serves as a staff reporter at Centrist Nation TV, where she covers stories on social issues, culture, and youth perspectives. She also works remotely as an administrative assistant for a U.S.-based Home-Health Aid Agency, gaining valuable experience in global coordination and professional management.

Through her diverse roles, Nowshin blends media insight, cultural advocacy, and administrative expertise to contribute meaningfully to BSCF’s mission of empowering youth, fostering social awareness, and building bridges between Bangladesh and the global community.

coreTeam.name: Avik Arefin
coreTeam.bio:
Meet Avik Arefin, a skilled mechatronics engineering student from Bangladesh. He is pursuing a BSc in Mechatronics Engineering at Rajshahi University of Engineering and Technology. 

Avik works on exciting projects that mix software, robotics, and real-world tech. For Aethes Corporation in the USA, he builds the Armor Gold Secure Locker App using Flutter. He has also launched websites like rupsa.club, bscf.dev, and logistica4u.com.

In competitions, Avik shines with his team Cortex Robotics. They took first place in Bangladesh for the 2025 JAXA-NASA Kibo Robot Challenge and second in 2024. He was a finalist in Huawei's Seeds for the Future Bangladesh 2024 and helped Team Bangladesh reach top 10 in the Tech4Good event in China. He joins many university and national tech and business contests.

He is an assistant director at Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum, he is involved with Logistica at logistica4u.com, and Cortex Technologies at cortexrobotics.net. His certifications cover Supervised Machine Learning from Andrew Ng on Coursera, top 10 in Huawei Seeds for the Future 2024, and industrial training at Forbes Marshall in March 2024.

Avik aims to blend engineering, coding, and innovation to solve problems in real life and consumer focused tech.

coreTeam.name: Tayba Tabassum Ahmed Rafa
coreTeam.bio: Meet Tayba Tabassum Ahmed Rafa, a passionate anthropology graduate skilled in cultural research, qualitative analysis, and ethnographic studies from Rajshahi, Bangladesh. She holds a BSS in Anthropology from the University of Rajshahi. From late 2023 to early 2024, she served as a research assistant at Surch Bangladesh, supporting data collection and analysis. In Project Sware-O with Team Nirvana, she helped enhance digital literacy for youth in Rajshahi to build a smarter Bangladesh. Her achievements include attending a bootcamp on climate science and negotiations by Manusher Jonno Foundation, participating in Youth Policy Summit 2024, Policy Camp-X, and gender training by Youth for Policy. As public relations officer for Youth for Policy's Rajshahi District Committee, general member of Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum, junior executive at Rajshahi University Model United Nations Association, and organizing secretary at Mymensingh Zilla Somity, she fosters policy discussions and cultural understanding. Tayba seeks to apply her expertise in social research, community development, and policy analysis for projects that advance social equity and sustainable development.

coreTeam.name: Prity Dutta Roy
coreTeam.bio: Meet Prity Dutta Roy, an aspiring educator and active community member with a passion for music, art, and liberty advocacy from Kishoreganj, Bangladesh. She is pursuing a BEd in the Institute of Education and Research at the University of Rajshahi. Since March 2022, Prity has been a member of the Rajshahi University Music Club, engaging in performances and cultural activities. As Local Coordinator for Students for Liberty in the South-East Asia Region, Rajshahi, since May 2023, she promotes pro-liberty ideas among students. Her strengths include singing, teamwork, and art, complemented by proficiency in English, Bengali, and Hindi. Prity aims to leverage her educational background and extracurricular involvement to foster creative and inclusive learning environments.

coreTeam.name: Najib Chowdhury
coreTeam.bio: Meet Najib Chowdhury, a versatile engineering undergraduate and aspiring Machine Learning Engineer from Rajshahi, Bangladesh. He is pursuing a BSc in Mechatronics Engineering at Rajshahi University of Engineering & Technology since 2023. Najib's projects include a real-time DC motor control system using Arduino, PID controller, and MATLAB/Simulink for tuning, a dual-parameter monitoring system with machine learning models for event classification, and an autonomous firefighting robot integrating sensors and ML for fire detection and response. In early 2025, he interned at BSRM Steels Limited, gaining hands-on experience in industrial power systems, automation with PLC and SCADA, and steel manufacturing processes. As an executive member of the Robotic Society of RUET since 2023, he organizes workshops on robotics and automation. He volunteered at TechMayhem in 2023 for event logistics and support. In early 2025, as a member of Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum, he led research on youth socio-cultural data, produced reports, and helped organize the 1st BSCF Astronomy Camp, where he mentored students on AI and robotics fundamentals. Skilled in C++, Python, PyTorch, TensorFlow, MATLAB, embedded systems like STM32, and tools like ROS and Git, Najib thrives in AI, automation, and cross-disciplinary innovation.

coreTeam.name: Safiat Rahaman Sifat
coreTeam.bio:
Meet Safiat Rahaman (Sifat), a dynamic communications and policy professional with strong leadership in youth-led NGOs, think tanks, and CSR efforts from Dhaka, Bangladesh. He is enrolling in a Bachelor of Science at North South University in Fall 2025. Skilled in grassroots operations, strategic partnerships, and program design, Safiat co-founded Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum (BSCF) and seeks remote roles to drive communication, leadership, and policy change.

From 2023 to March 2025 at Youth School for Social Entrepreneurship (YSSE), he advanced from Intern to Senior Associate in Communications, managing 134 interns, designing competitions like ThinkTank and National Case Competition, and creating content for visibility. He earned Best Intern for three months and a Gold/Star Badge. As Co-Founder and Chief of Grassroots at BSCF since 2024, he launched the Future STEM Leaders Programme (Cohort-1 with 36 interns), built corporate partnerships, and directed volunteer mobilization, events, and outreach. At Youth Policy Forum (YPF) as Youth Engagement Lead and Policy Associate since 2023, he contributed to policy dialogues, edited a behavioral economics paper, organized youth events, and won Best Contributor for engagement.

Safiat leads the YED Project at Rajbari Socio-Cultural Club (RSCF) since 2023, one of 16 organizations in a major initiative, organizing debates, workshops, and National Bangla Utsab for three years. Since 2025, he develops content and mentors at Gamephone Academy and LCOY Bangladesh, focusing on digital learning, climate training, and UNFCCC policy dialogues. As Vice President of Communications at Debating Club of Rouf College from 2022 to 2024, he onboarded over 550 members and coordinated festivals.

In education and facilitation, he created English content for platforms like 10 Minute School and British Council, delivered workshops for IDLC Finance Olympiad (150,000 participants), and tutored in Biology and English. Achievements ... [truncated]

coreTeam.name: Ahnaf Tahsin Monmoy
coreTeam.bio:
Meet Ahnaf Tahsin Monmoy, a dedicated undergraduate student in Accounting and Information Systems at the University of Rajshahi. With a strong passion for blending technology with finance, Ahnaf focuses on using innovative tools to improve financial reporting, decision-making, and data-driven solutions. His analytical mindset and problem-solving skills drive his interest in data analytics, financial modeling, and tech-enhanced business processes.

As Founder and CEO of Brogotflix, a streaming service, since June 2022, Ahnaf leads operations in digital media management. He also co-founded Mobile Guru, a shop at Jamuna Future Park, in May 2022, where he applies his financial management expertise.

Ahnaf is an associate member of the Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum since October 2023 and the Rajshahi University Professional Development Club since November 2023. His skills include teamwork, leadership, effective communication, critical thinking, MS Office proficiency, graphic design, and digital media management. Fluent in English and native in Bangla, he aims to contribute at the intersection of technology, accounting, and business to optimize processes and drive innovation.

coreTeam.name: Abdullah Al Hossain Sami
coreTeam.bio: Meet Abdullah Al Hossain Sami, a motivated and creative student with a passion for music, cultural activities, and leadership from Rajshahi, Bangladesh. He is pursuing a BBA in Accounting and Information Systems at the University of Rajshahi. Since 2025, as assistant director of the cultural sub-committee at Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum, he plans and coordinates programs, guides teams, organizes workshops, and promotes student engagement. As lead guitarist for Hermes The Band since 2021, he contributes to performances, music composition, and event coordination. He also serves as composer and lead guitarist for Isle Of Bones since 2021, creating original music and leading rehearsals for live events. From 2020 to 2021, he was project management officer at Cultural Aethetes, organizing programs focused on students' co-curricular activities and creativity. Skilled in team coordination, event management, public speaking, music composition, photography, and content creation, Sami aims to grow through socio-cultural initiatives that enhance personal development and community involvement.

coreTeam.name: Zarin Roshni
coreTeam.bio:
Meet Zarin Roshni, a motivated student and emerging leader in communication and education from Rajshahi, Bangladesh. Zarin seeks opportunities in project management to apply her organizational and leadership skills for team success.

Since July 2025, she serves as Assistant Director in the Communication Department at Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum (BSCF), a non-profit focused on promoting science, culture, and social awareness. There, she engages students in leadership, research, and community activities. From October 2021 to December 2022, Zarin worked as a journalist for Duronto Shishu-Kishor Potrika, a children's magazine that uncovers young talents, inspires creativity, and showcases the nation through kids' perspectives. In early 2024, she was a Poetry Instructor and Early Childhood Educator at Alo Academy, using play-based methods to teach dance, music, recitation, and art for holistic child development.

She participated in BSCF's Astronomy Camp, involving observational astronomy, scientific talks, and team space research. She looks to build on her experience in fostering creativity and community impact.

coreTeam.name: S.M. Labib
coreTeam.bio: Meet S.M. Labib, a skilled graphic and UI/UX designer from Dhaka, Bangladesh. With over two years of experience, he founded a creative agency and serves as Assistant Director of the Creative Team at Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum (BSCF). There, he works to engage young people in social, cultural, and service-oriented activities to build an environmentally conscious, skilled, and resilient generation. From 2023 to 2024, as Junior UX Designer at Rajbari Socio-Cultural Forum (RSCF), he contributed to sustainable development by involving youth in social, cultural, and educational activities. Between 2020 and 2022, Labib was a Graphic Designer at Career Club of Rajbari (CCR), helping raise career awareness among Rajbari students. He is studying a Bachelor's at Shanto Marium University of Creative Technology since 2023, after earning his Higher Secondary Certificate from Rajbari Govt College. Proficient in Photoshop, Figma, Illustrator, and After Effects, Labib helps startups, agencies, and coaches bring ideas to life through cinematic visuals, smooth UI animations, and high-impact explainer videos.
